title: Founding Engineer
description:
Built and owned full-stack and AI product work in a founding-stage environment, taking features from early product discussions through implementation, testing, deployment, and iteration. Focused on agent-based AI systems that are reliable, production-oriented, and grounded in real user needs.
- Built infrastructure for long-running AI agent workflows, including state management, failure recovery, and reproducible execution
- Shipped full-stack product features end to end across backend systems and user-facing product surfaces
- Worked closely with customer-facing and CRM-oriented teammates to turn workflow friction and user feedback into internal tools and product improvements
- Built CI/CD, deployment, and testing systems to improve engineering velocity and release reliability
- Reduced agent initialization time from 30+ seconds to under 2 seconds
- Helped shape roadmap priorities, engineering standards, and technical direction in an early-stage team

title: Data Engineer, Full Stack Developer
description:
Worked across AI systems, full-stack engineering, and internal tooling in support of Gray Whale's product and platform initiatives. The role focused on agent orchestration, RAG and tool-calling evaluation, structured data pipelines, and model-ready backend workflows.
- Built an internal multi-agent orchestration system using local LLMs, later incorporating Gray Whale's proprietary model as the orchestration layer for agent decision-making
- Evaluated tool-calling and RAG workflows across latency, accuracy, and token usage to improve efficiency and reduce inference cost
- Built pipelines that transformed unstructured input data into structured JSON for training and downstream model workflows
- Developed audio transcription infrastructure to capture and ingest live-streamed data for downstream AI use cases
- Worked across product, systems, and backend development in a fast-moving startup environment

title: AI Engineer & Product Consultant
description:
Worked on AI-powered product development for surf forecasting and recommendation experiences, with a focus on assistant quality, observability, and early-stage product iteration.
- Built and improved LangSmith-based observability workflows to trace model behavior, troubleshoot failures, and improve reliability
- Helped shape LLM-powered forecasting and recommendation features by translating product goals into implementation direction and evaluation criteria
- Ran prompt, retrieval, and assistant workflow experiments to improve product quality and usefulness
- Contributed to product design and early execution in a fast-moving startup environment
- Supported broader growth efforts, including marketing strategy and intern coordination

title: Teaching Assistant
description:
Served as a Teaching Assistant across AI and information systems courses in the Computer Science and Technology Information Management departments, supporting 150+ students per course through teaching, mentorship, grading, and course operations.
- Supported CSE 140 (Artificial Intelligence), helping teach AI fundamentals including search, game playing, machine learning, and symbolic and neural methods
- Served as Lead TA for TIM courses in Systems Analysis and Design and Business Information Systems
- Held office hours, led discussions, and supported students on SQL, relational databases, Agile/SCRUM, UML, and enterprise systems
- Managed assessments, autograders, rubrics, Gradescope workflows, and coordination across TA, tutor, and grader teams
- Mentored student project teams and occasionally guest lectured on business-IT integration topics

title: Full Stack Developer & Product Consultant
description:
Worked across full-stack product development and internal tooling for a growing marketplace app, contributing to both user-facing features and operational workflows in an early-stage startup environment.
- Helped grow the app to 20,000+ users through feature development and product improvements
- Built GPT-powered tools for marketing outreach and customer communication
- Developed personalized alerts and spreadsheet-integrated workflows to improve engagement and team efficiency
- Contributed across the stack to expand platform functionality and support startup growth
- Helped implement Agile processes to speed up development and better align with user needs

title: Surfing Instructor
description:
Provided private and group surf instruction for students across a range of ages and experience levels, with an emphasis on safety, communication, and personalized coaching in dynamic ocean conditions.
- Delivered tailored instruction based on individual skill level, confidence, and learning style
- Taught foundational surf technique, ocean awareness, and safe navigation practices
- Created a supportive learning environment that helped students build confidence and progress quickly
- Managed real-time safety and decision-making in changing outdoor conditions
- Strengthened communication, coaching, and adaptability through hands-on instruction

title: Peer Advisor
description:
Provided front-line academic advising support for a diverse student population, including four-year and transfer students, helping them navigate university systems and connect with the resources they needed to succeed.
- Supported students with class enrollment, graduation planning, petitions, and general academic navigation
- Helped translate university processes and requirements into clear, actionable guidance
- Directed students and visitors to the appropriate departments, faculty, and campus resources
- Built strong communication and problem-solving skills while working with students from a wide range of backgrounds and needs
- Contributed to a supportive advising environment focused on accessibility, responsiveness, and student success

title: Maintenance Worker
description:
Supported the upkeep of campus housing and facilities through maintenance, repair work, and responsive service for students and staff. Worked in a hands-on role that required reliability, attention to detail, and clear communication in a fast-paced environment.
- Performed maintenance and repair work across dorms and campus buildings, including room repairs and plumbing-related tasks
- Responded to maintenance requests from students and staff to help maintain safe, functional living and learning spaces
- Tracked completed work and maintained service records with attention to detail and organization
- Built practical problem-solving skills in a role centered on responsiveness, accountability, and day-to-day operations

title: Programs Assistant
description:
Coordinated and facilitated student-facing programs during a period that included the transition from remote to in-person campus life. Worked across event planning, communication, community engagement, and operational support to help build inclusive programming and strengthen student participation.
- Coordinated virtual and in-person programs that supported student engagement and community continuity during the transition back to campus
- Designed and executed events focused on environmental justice, cultural appreciation, and the college theme of Environment and Society
- Developed marketing materials across social media, newsletters, posters, and multimedia content to increase awareness and participation
- Collaborated with Welcome Leaders and supported event execution during Welcome Week to help new students integrate socially and academically
- Served as a liaison between staff and student communities, gathering feedback and adapting programming to evolving student needs
- Contributed to resource planning, budget tracking, and event documentation to support sustainable program operations

company: Club Ed Surf School and Camps
title: Surfing Instructor
description:
Provided beginner-to-intermediate surf instruction with an emphasis on individualized feedback, safe ocean practices, and reliable lesson execution. Supported both teaching and day-to-day operations in a hands-on, outdoor environment.
- Taught surf fundamentals to beginner and intermediate students, adapting instruction to skill level and confidence
- Emphasized ocean safety, situational awareness, and sound technique throughout lessons
- Managed surf equipment setup, maintenance, and transport between the office and lesson locations
- Supported daily lesson logistics to help ensure smooth operations and consistent student experiences
- Built communication, adaptability, and operational reliability in a fast-paced instructional setting

title: Skateboarding Instructor
description:
Designed and delivered individualized skateboarding instruction for students with different experience levels, with a focus on progressive skill-building, confidence, and creating an encouraging learning environment. Also supported event logistics for community programming connected to the local skateboarding scene.
- Taught skateboarding lessons tailored to each student's skill level, pace, and learning style
- Used hands-on, progressive instruction to build confidence and create an inclusive, supportive environment
- Helped students develop foundational technique, safety awareness, and consistency through structured practice
- Supported logistics and event setup for the Clash at Clairemont skateboarding demo, featuring professional skaters including Tony Hawk and Andy Macdonald
- Strengthened communication, coaching, and community-building skills through youth instruction and event support

title: Graphic Designer
description:
Created custom visual design work for a range of clients, translating ideas into polished graphics for apparel, school events, and branded materials. Focused on clear communication, fast iteration, and producing designs tailored to each client's goals and audience.
- Designed custom visuals for apparel, school events, and promotional use
- Translated client ideas into polished, high-impact designs through iterative feedback and refinement
- Created custom apparel graphics including Cross Country team shirts, a Triple Jump letterman jacket design, and the La Jolla High School Senior vs. Faculty Basketball Game t-shirt
- Balanced creativity with client needs, timelines, and practical production constraints
- Built early experience in visual communication, client collaboration, and independent project execution
